definition: The same group of related fields repeatedly appears together, signaling they likely belong in a single object.
ruleOfThumb: If the same parameters travel as a pack, give the pack a name.

citation.author: Martin Fowler
citation.bookTitle: Refactoring: Improving the Design of Existing Code

questions.prompt: What design signal stands out in these APIs?

options.text: The same address fields appear together across multiple functions, suggesting they should be modeled as one address type
options.explanation: Repeated parameter groups indicate missing domain structure. A dedicated Address object improves readability, validation reuse, and call-site safety.

options.text: The recipient name should be optional so shipping flows that only need a physical address don't have to supply it
options.explanation: Optionality could be discussed, but it does not explain the repeated field cluster.

options.text: Function names like createShipment and estimateShipping should start with verbs to satisfy team style guides and improve readability
options.explanation: Naming style is not the key structural issue shown.

options.text: All three functions should live as methods on a single utility class to keep related shipping operations together
options.explanation: Class vs function placement does not solve the repeated field bundle.

questions.prompt: Why do bugs often appear when developers add country support to this code?

options.text: Customer identity fields are scattered as repeated argument lists, so adding one new field requires synchronized signature changes everywhere
options.explanation: With repeated parameter packs, one schema change ripples through many methods and call sites. A CustomerContact value object localizes that change.

options.text: The issue is that save and index should be async functions returning Promises
options.explanation: Async behavior may matter at runtime, but it is separate from repeated data-shape coupling.

options.text: Phone number should be removed from contact flows since modern systems route all communications through email
options.explanation: Domain choice about channels is independent of the repeated argument clump.

options.text: A base class with these four parameters as protected fields would remove the problem
options.explanation: Inheritance can still leave repeated constructor and method signatures. The issue is missing cohesive data type.

questions.prompt: Which version is more likely to remain stable as address requirements evolve?

options.text: Version A — repeated field packs force broad signature churn whenever address shape changes
options.explanation: Any new field like apartmentNumber requires editing each signature and call site. Version A evolves the address model in one place.

options.text: Version B — passing objects hides required fields and should be avoided for critical business flows
options.explanation: A typed object makes required fields explicit and cohesive, especially for shared domain bundles like addresses.
